content.full_text:
May 7, 1976

Mr. George Estrada  
517 Adams Street  
Santa Ana, California 92707  

Re: Your Letter of April 23, 1976  

Dear Mr. Estrada:

Your letter has been assigned to me for reply. In response to your specific questions, I trust the following will be useful.

1. Are appointive members of city boards and commissions covered by the Political Reform Act of 1974?

A. Yes, pursuant to local Conflict of Interest Codes, so long as the board or commission is a "decision-making" board or commission within the meaning of Proposition Nine.

2. Can an elected official be removed from office for a violation of this Act?

A. Locally elected public officials, if convicted for violation of local Conflict of Interest Codes, probably could not be removed for the duration of their current term. However, once convicted, §91002 might be applicable to prevent re-election.

"91002. No person convicted of a misdemeanor under this title shall be a candidate for any elective office or act as a lobbyist for a period of four years following the date of the conviction unless the court at the time of sentencing specifically determines that this provision shall not be applicable. A plea of nolo contendere shall be deemed a conviction for purposes of this section. Any person violating this section is guilty of a felony."
